raw.subject: Built something fun for a customer today, huh? Something I noticed about J&M Mobile Auto Repairs
raw.body:
Hi,

I was browsing through your Facebook page and I was impressed by the Spyder Dune Buggy you built for a new customer. That kind of unique project must be exciting for your team!

But I noticed that you don't have your own website yet - as a mobile repair service, I assume you get a lot of calls and bookings that might get lost without a dedicated online presence.

I took a look at the photos and posts you share on Facebook and thought it would be a shame to let that great content go to waste. I sketched out a simple homepage idea that brings together the services you offer, your awesome projects, a contact section, and even a map to help customers find you in Killeen.

https://sites.damianbasilio.dev/preview/j-m-mobile-auto-repairs-killeen

I'd love to chat with you about making this idea a reality with a straightforward project and a flat fee for local businesses. If you're interested in learning more, feel free to reply to this email or give me a quick call.
